PickAlpha Weekend View-

Berkshire’s first big Abel-era deal is a housing-cycle bet. Reuters reports Berkshire Hathaway agreed to buy Taylor Morrison in an all-cash transaction valued at about $8.5B, paying $72.50/share — a roughly 24% premium — and pushing deeper into site-built homebuilding alongside Clayton Homes.  

Tickers: $BRK.B $TMHC $DHI

Our view is this is classic Berkshire: use permanent capital when a cyclical industry is under pressure, not when the tape is clean. The deal is not large relative to Berkshire’s balance sheet, but it tells you Greg Abel is willing to deploy capital into durable real assets where scale, land, financing, and patience matter more than near-term housing data.

$OPAD   M&A headline hitting the tape. 
 
$TMHC is getting acquired by Berkshire Hathaway in an all cash deal. Price at $72.50 per share, valuing the company around $8.5B total and ~$6.8B equity value. That’s roughly a 24% premium baked in. 
 
What matters here is not just the premium, but Buffett stepping back into housing exposure through a large scale builder at a time when rates and demand dynamics are still shifting. 
 
Deal flow like this tends to reprice the whole sector, not just the target. Watching housing stocks closely on sentiment spillover.

body:
Big M&A headline hitting the tape. 
 
$TMHC is getting acquired by Berkshire Hathaway in an all cash deal. Price at $72.50 per share, valuing the company around $8.5B total and ~$6.8B equity value. That’s roughly a 24% premium baked in. 
 
What matters here is not just the premium, but Buffett stepping back into housing exposure through a large scale builder at a time when rates and demand dynamics are still shifting. 
 
Deal flow like this tends to reprice the whole sector, not just the target. Watching homebuilders closely on sentiment spillover.

Berkshire Hathaway to Acquire Taylor Morrison Home Corporation for $8.5 Billion  
 
"Taylor Morrison Home Corporation (NYSE:TMHC) and Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE:BRK, BRK.B)) jointly announced today that they have reached a definitive agreement for Berkshire Hathaway to acquire Taylor Morrison for $72.50 per common share in cash, representing a total equity value for Taylor Morrison of approximately $6.8 billion and total enterprise value of approximately $8.5 billion. The acquisition price represents a 24% premium to Taylor Morrison's latest closing price of $58.50 on May 29, 2026."

bearish.body: Taylor Morrison experienced a 28% decline in home closings revenue and a 14% decrease in net sales orders, alongside an increased SG&A ratio, reflecting market challenges and reduced demand.

bullish.body: Taylor Morrison's backlog grew 23% sequentially, driven by increased to-be-built orders and strong resort lifestyle segment sales, while maintaining robust liquidity and executing share repurchases.

summary.body:
• Reported GAAP EPS of $1.03 down -51.18% YoY
• Reported revenue of $1.39B down -26.84% YoY
• Taylor Morrison reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance, including approximately 11,000 home closings, an average closing price between $580,000 and $590,000, and approximately $2B in homebuilding land investment.

bearish.body: Home closings revenue for Taylor Morrison decreased approximately 28% to $1.3B, driven by an approximately 26% decline in closings volume and an approximately 4% decrease in average closing price.
Taylor Morrison's SG&A as a percentage of home closings revenue increased to 11.4% from 9.7% a year ago, as the reduction in SG&A dollar spend was offset by lower home closings revenue.
Net sales orders for Taylor Morrison decreased approximately 14% to 2,914, and the monthly sales pace was down from 3.3 in the first quarter of 2025 to 2.7 per community.

bullish.body: Taylor Morrison's backlog grew 23% sequentially to 3,465 homes, with net orders outpacing closings and the mix of to-be-built orders increasing to 38% from 28%.
The resort lifestyle segment was the only consumer segment for Taylor Morrison to grow year over year, driven by a 9% increase in Esplanade sales.
Taylor Morrison maintained strong financial flexibility, ending the quarter with approximately $1.6B in total liquidity and repurchasing 2.5M common shares for $150M.

Taylor Morrison Remains A Solid Candidate For The Seasonal Trade On Home Builders 
https://seekingalpha.com/article/4875181?gt=883c8956eaaead83 
* Taylor Morrison remains range-bound, offering attractive risk/reward for seasonal trades despite tepid 2026 guidance and challenging market conditions. 
* TMHC projects 2026 home closings down 15% and revenue down ~17%, yet maintains industry-high margins and a $400M buyback commitment. 
* Management is retrenching to core markets, limiting land investment in price-sensitive submarkets to protect margins amid elevated inventory and high incentives. 
* TMHC’s strong balance sheet, 1.0x book value, and relative peer outperformance support a covered call strategy or opportunistic share accumulation near $58.

body:
$LABU $LABD …. Fundamentals & Other Factors 
 
US Crude Oil has dumped hard on the backs of deal with Iran.  Oil Spot price closed the week at $86.73.  Oil and 10-yr yield charts are identical so you can see how much correlation market is putting into oil and its effects on inflation expectations.  CPI and PPI were hotter but somehow PCE did not show as much inflation as the other two metrics suggested. 
 
10-yr closed the week at 4.44%.  Its 50 MA sits just below at 4.38%.  Will it bounce there or drop harder?  The answer lies in how WH projects the deal with Iran.  However, you can see RSI on the yields is near a critical level and so it is on the oil chart.  Perhaps a bounce next, who knows?  10-2 yield spreads moved significantly lower and closed the week at 44 bps. 
   
BTC is in a bit of trouble here.  BTC got supported by its 50 DMA rising support but then broke below it and out of the channel/LT Bear flag it was in.  This may revisit the 60K mark likely if the markets turn sour. 
 
Contd….

body:
$LABU $LABD …. Elliot Wave Perspective 
 
Here we go again.  Back at the top side of the channel that looks like a bull flag to me.  However, some say the breakout (if it happens) generally is the size of the flag pole from the low end of the flag.  If XBI breaks out to the upside, that gives me 146 on XBI as topping level (Size of the pole = 21 pts from low end of the flag = 125 on XBI). 
 
Also, EW principles dictate that the final 5th intermediate wave in the final Major Wave 5 could hit 1.382 fib level off of intermediate IV wave, and at most 1.618 level as a stretch.  As you can see in the chart below, my final targets for XBI are 144.50 and 148 for Extended levels.  However, for all this to come true, first XBI has to break-through the flag channel, which it hasn’t yet.  We have exhibited extensive resistance at the 138-139 levels in the past. 
 
June is a bearish month generally and we have bearish divergences developing on the weekly chart too.  Let’s see how June shapes up. 
 
Contd….

data.text: The UEV platform was touted by management as a Model T moment. For that reason, the EV pickup reveal date could be 10/01/2026, the 118th year anniversary of the original Model T reveal. That's just 4 months from now.

The very first Ford model to ride on the brand new Universal Electric Vehicle (UEV) Platform is slated to enter production in 2027 at the Louisville Assembly plant, and last month, CEO Jim Farley revealed that it will make its long-awaited debut "later this year." Turns out, that's also when customers will be able to place an order for one. 
 
"We're confident on our launch plan. In fact, we're right on track to share our plans with dealers and take customer orders later this year," Andrew Frick, president of Ford Blue and Ford Model e, said during the automaker's Q1 2026 earnings call with investors. "And what we're really excited about is some of the EV market trends that we're seeing and the EV volume really heading towards the affordable space, which really favors this affordable UEV platform positioning us right in the heart of the market. So we're really pleased with that."
